text:
occupational health doctors do not always know
the consequences of IBD in the work context; if you decide
to tell them, you should not hesitate to inform them precisely about your fatigue,
travel times during flare-ups and the possibilities
for adjustments or a change of position,
if any exist within your workplace
or company.

M Talking to management :
= There is clearly a risk in disclosing this at
the time of hiring (except of course if you are
hired under an RQTH) or during the probationary
period. The employer, worried about the impacts of
this situation, can refuse to hire you or end the probationary period without giving the real reasons
since they do not have to justify their decision.
= On the other hand, you may be obliged to tell
your management if the disease evolves with
long or frequent absences or if you wish, for
example, to obtain adjustments to your hours or
position. Again, the disease may be named or not.
